briefdescription.para: The default SDO timeout (in milliseconds) for the NMT 'boot slave' and 'check configuration' processes.
The CAN identifier used for NMT commands.
The NMT command specifier 'start'.
The NMT command specifier 'stop'.
The NMT command specifier 'enter pre-operational'.
The NMT command specifier 'reset node'.
The NMT command specifier 'reset communication'.
The NMT state 'boot-up'.
The NMT state 'stopped'.
The NMT state 'operational'.
The NMT sub-state 'reset application'.
The NMT sub-state 'reset communication'.
The NMT state 'pre-operational'.
The mask to get/set the toggle bit from an NMT state.
The CAN identifier used for both node guarding and heartbeat monitoring.
An NMT error control event occurred.
An NMT error control event was resolved.
The NMT error control event state.
An NMT error control timeout event.
An NMT error control state change event.
The NMT error control event reason.
An NMT ECSS redundancy manager bus switch.
An NMT ECSS redundancy manager no-master operation.
The NMT ECSS redundancy event reason.
The NMT error control event state.
The NMT error control event reason.
The NMT ECSS redundancy event reason.

memberdef.definition: typedef void co_nmt_cs_ind_t(co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t cs, void *data)
memberdef.name: co_nmt_cs_ind_t

briefdescription.para: The type of a CANopen NMT command indication function, invoked when an NMT command is received (and after the state switch has occurred).

detaileddescription.para: Since the indication function is invoked during an NMT state transition, it is NOT safe to invoke co_nmt_cs_ind() (or co_nmt_cs_req() with the node-ID of the master) from this function.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the NMT command specifier (one of CO_NMT_CS_START, CO_NMT_CS_STOP, CO_NMT_CS_ENTER_PREOP, CO_NMT_CS_RESET_NODE or CO_NMT_CS_RESET_COMM).
a pointer to user-specified data.

memberdef.definition: typedef void co_nmt_ng_ind_t(co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t id, co_nmt_ec_state_t state, co_nmt_ec_reason_t reason, void *data)
memberdef.argsstring: (co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t id, co_nmt_ec_state_t state, co_nmt_ec_reason_t reason, void *data)
memberdef.name: co_nmt_ng_ind_t

briefdescription.para: The type of a CANopen NMT node guarding indication function, invoked when a node guarding event occurs (see sections 7.2.8.2.2.1 and 7.2.8.3.2.1 in CiA 301 version 4.2.0).

detaileddescription.para: The default handler invokes co_nmt_on_ng().

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the node-ID (in the range [1..127]).
indicates whether the event occurred (CO_NMT_EC_OCCURRED) or was resolved (CO_NMT_EC_RESOLVED).
indicates whether the event occurred because of a timeout (CO_NMT_EC_TIMEOUT) or an unexpected state change (CO_NMT_EC_STATE).
a pointer to user-specified data.

memberdef.definition: typedef void co_nmt_lg_ind_t(co_nmt_t *nmt, co_nmt_ec_state_t state, void *data)
memberdef.name: co_nmt_lg_ind_t

briefdescription.para: The type of a CANopen NMT life guarding indication function, invoked when a life guarding event occurs (see section 7.2.8.2.2.2 in CiA 301 version 4.2.0).

detaileddescription.para: The default handler invokes co_nmt_on_lg().

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT slave service.
indicates whether the event occurred (CO_NMT_EC_OCCURRED) or was resolved (CO_NMT_EC_RESOLVED).
a pointer to user-specified data.

memberdef.definition: typedef void co_nmt_hb_ind_t(co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t id, co_nmt_ec_state_t state, co_nmt_ec_reason_t reason, void *data)
memberdef.argsstring: (co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t id, co_nmt_ec_state_t state, co_nmt_ec_reason_t reason, void *data)
memberdef.name: co_nmt_hb_ind_t

briefdescription.para: The type of a CANopen NMT heartbeat indication function, invoked when a heartbeat event occurs (see sections 7.2.8.2.2.3 and 7.2.8.3.2.2 in CiA 301 version 4.2.0).

detaileddescription.para: The default handler invokes co_nmt_on_hb().

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the node-ID (in the range [1..127]).
indicates whether the event occurred (CO_NMT_EC_OCCURRED) or was resolved (CO_NMT_EC_RESOLVED). Note that heartbeat state change events only occur and are never resolved.
indicates whether the event occurred because of a timeout (CO_NMT_EC_TIMEOUT) or a state change (CO_NMT_EC_STATE).
a pointer to user-specified data.

memberdef.definition: typedef void co_nmt_ecss_rdn_ind_t(co_nmt_t *nmt, uint_least8_t bus_id, co_nmt_ecss_rdn_reason_t reason, void *data)
memberdef.name: co_nmt_ecss_rdn_ind_t

briefdescription.para: The type of a CANopen NMT redundancy indication function, invoked when a redundancy event occurs.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the active CAN bus identifier.
indicates whether the event occurred because of a bus switch (CO_NMT_ECSS_RDN_BUS_SWITCH) or entering no-master operation mode (CO_NMT_ECSS_RDN_NO_MASTER).
a pointer to user-specified data.

memberdef.definition: typedef void co_nmt_st_ind_t(co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t id, co_unsigned8_t st, void *data)
memberdef.name: co_nmt_st_ind_t

briefdescription.para: The type of a CANopen NMT state change indication function, invoked when a state change is detected by the node guarding or heartbeat protocol.

detaileddescription.para: This indication is an extension of the boot-up event indication (sections 7.2.8.2.3.1 and 7.2.8.3.3 in CiA 301 version 4.2.0) since it reports all state changes, not just the boot-up event. The default handler invokes co_nmt_on_st().

This indication function is also invoked by an NMT master/slave service to notify the user of its own state changes. In that case it is NOT safe to invoke co_nmt_cs_ind() (or co_nmt_cs_req() with the node-ID of the master) from this function.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the node-ID (in the range [1..127]).
the state of the node (excluding the toggle bit).
a pointer to user-specified data.

briefdescription.para: The type of a CANopen LSS request function, invoked by an NMT master before booting the slaves (see Fig.

detaileddescription.para: 1 in CiA 302-2 version 4.1.0). This function MUST cause co_nmt_lss_con() to be invoked once the LSS process completes.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
a pointer to an LSS master service.
a pointer to user-specified data.

memberdef.definition: typedef void co_nmt_boot_ind_t(co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t id, co_unsigned8_t st, char es, void *data)
memberdef.argsstring: (co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t id, co_unsigned8_t st, char es, void *data)
memberdef.name: co_nmt_boot_ind_t

briefdescription.para: The type of a CANopen NMT 'boot slave' indication function, invoked when the 'boot slave' process completes.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the node-ID of the slave (in the range [1..127]).
the state of the node (including the toggle bit).
the error status (in the range ['A'..'O'], or 0 on success).
a pointer to user-specified data.

memberdef.definition: typedef void co_nmt_cfg_ind_t(co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t id, co_csdo_t *sdo, void *data)
memberdef.name: co_nmt_cfg_ind_t

briefdescription.para: The type of a CANopen NMT 'update configuration' indication function, invoked when a configuration request is received.

detaileddescription.para: This function MUST cause co_nmt_cfg_res() to be invoked once the 'update configuration' step completes (with success or failure).

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the node-ID of the slave (in the range [1..127]).
a pointer to a Client-SDO connected to the slave.
a pointer to user-specified data.

memberdef.definition: typedef void co_nmt_cfg_con_t(co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t id, co_unsigned32_t ac, void *data)
memberdef.name: co_nmt_cfg_con_t

briefdescription.para: The type of a CANopen NMT 'configuration request' confirmation callback function, invoked when a configuration request completes (with success or failure).

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the node-ID of the slave (in the range [1..127]).
the SDO abort code (0 on success).
a pointer to user-specified data.

memberdef.definition: typedef void co_nmt_sdo_ind_t(co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t id, co_unsigned16_t idx, co_unsigned8_t subidx, size_t size, size_t nbyte, void *data)
memberdef.argsstring: (co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t id, co_unsigned16_t idx, co_unsigned8_t subidx, size_t size, size_t nbyte, void *data)
memberdef.name: co_nmt_sdo_ind_t

briefdescription.para: The type of an SDO request progress indication function, invoked by a CANopen NMT master to notify the user of the progress of the an SDO upload/download request during the 'boot slave' process.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the node-ID of the slave (in the range [1..127]).
the object index.
the object sub-index.
The total size (in bytes) of the value being uploaded/downloaded.
The number of bytes already uploaded/downloaded.
a pointer to user-specified data.

memberdef.definition: typedef void co_nmt_sync_ind_t(co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t cnt, void *data)
memberdef.name: co_nmt_sync_ind_t

briefdescription.para: The type of a SYNC indication function, invoked by co_nmt_on_sync() after PDOs are transmitted/processed upon reception/transmission of a SYNC message.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the counter.
a pointer to user-specified data.

briefdescription.para: Configures heartbeat consumption for the specified node by updating CANopen object 1016 (Consumer heartbeat time).

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen device.
the node-ID (in the range [1..127]).
the heartbeat time (in milliseconds). If ms is 0, the heartbeat consumer is disabled.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or an SDO abort code on error.

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to a string describing an NMT boot error status.
Returns the alignment (in bytes) of the co_nmt_t structure.
Returns the size (in bytes) of the co_nmt_t structure.
Creates a new CANopen NMT master/slave service.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN network.
a pointer to a CANopen device.

simplesect.para: a pointer to a new NMT service, or NULL on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Destroys a CANopen NMT master/slave service.
Returns a pointer to the CAN network of an NMT master/slave service.
Returns a pointer to the CANopen device of an NMT master/slave service.
Retrieves the indication function invoked when an NMT command is received.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function invoked when an NMT command is received.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked.
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the indication function invoked when a node guarding event occurs.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function invoked when a node guarding event occurs.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked. If ind is NULL, the default indication function will be used (which invokes co_nmt_on_ng()).
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

memberdef.argsstring: (co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t id, co_nmt_ec_state_t state, co_nmt_ec_reason_t reason)
memberdef.name: co_nmt_on_ng

briefdescription.para: Implements the default behavior when a node guarding event occurs (see sections 7.2.8.2.2.1 and 7.2.8.3.2.1 in CiA 301 version 4.2.0).

detaileddescription.para: This function invokes co_nmt_comm_err_ind() when an event occurs.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the indication function invoked when a life guarding event occurs.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT slave service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function invoked when a life guarding event occurs.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT slave service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked. If ind is NULL, the default indication function will be used (which invokes co_nmt_on_lg()).
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Implements the default behavior when a life guarding event occurs (see section 7.2.8.2.2.2 in CiA 301 version 4.2.0).

detaileddescription.para: This function invokes co_nmt_comm_err_ind() when an event occurs.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the indication function invoked when a redundancy event occurs.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function invoked when a redundancy event occurs.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked (can be NULL).
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the indication function invoked when a heartbeat event occurs.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function invoked when a heartbeat event occurs.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked. If ind is NULL, the default indication function will be used (which invokes co_nmt_on_hb()).
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

memberdef.argsstring: (co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t id, co_nmt_ec_state_t state, co_nmt_ec_reason_t reason)
memberdef.name: co_nmt_on_hb

briefdescription.para: Implements the default behavior when a heartbeat event occurs (see sections 7.2.8.2.2.3 and 7.2.8.3.2.2 in CiA 301 version 4.2.0).

detaileddescription.para: This function invokes co_nmt_node_err_ind() or co_nmt_comm_err_ind() when a timeout event occurs, depending on whether the NMT service is a master or not (see co_nmt_is_master()).

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the indication function invoked when a state change is detected.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function invoked when a state change is detected.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked. If ind is NULL, the default indication function will be used (which invokes co_nmt_on_st()).
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Implements the default behavior when a state change is detected by the node guarding or heartbeat protocol.

detaileddescription.para: In case of a boot-up event (and the NMT service is a master), this function invokes co_nmt_boot_req() to boot the slave (see Fig. 13 in CiA 302-2).

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the request function invoked to perform LSS when booting an NMT master.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the request function invoked to perform LSS when booting an NMT master.

detaileddescription.para: Setting this function means LSS is required (see Fig. 1 in CiA 302-2 version 4.1.0).

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked.
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the indication function invoked when a CANopen NMT 'boot slave' process completes.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function invoked when a CANopen NMT 'boot slave' process completes.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked.
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the indication function invoked when a CANopen NMT 'configuration request' is received.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function invoked when a CANopen NMT 'configuration request' process is received.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked.
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the indication function used to notify the user of the progress of the current SDO download request during the CANopen NMT 'boot slave' process.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function used to notify the user of the progress of the current SDO download request during the CANopen NMT 'boot slave' process.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked.
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the indication function used to notify the user of the progress of the current SDO upload request during the CANopen NMT 'boot slave' process.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function used to notify the user of the progress of the current SDO upload request during the CANopen NMT 'boot slave' process.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked.
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the indication function invoked by co_nmt_on_sync() after all PDOs have been transmitted/processed.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function invoked by co_nmt_on_sync() after all PDOs have been transmitted/processed.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked.
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Implements the default behavior after a SYNC object is received or transmitted.

detaileddescription.para: First all synchronous Transmit-PDOs are sent with co_tpdo_sync(). Then all synchronous Receive-PDOs are actuated with co_rpdo_sync(). Finally the user-defined callback function set with co_nmt_set_sync_ind() is invoked.

memberdef.argsstring: (co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned16_t eec, co_unsigned8_t er, const co_unsigned8_t msef[5])
memberdef.name: co_nmt_on_err

briefdescription.para: Implements the default error handling behavior by generating an EMCY message with co_emcy_push() and invoking co_nmt_comm_err_ind() in case of a communication error (emergency error code 0x81xx).
Implements the default behavior when an event is indicated for an event-driven (asynchronous) Transmit-PDO by triggering the transmission of the PDO with co_tpdo_event().

detaileddescription.para: The transmission of PDOs can be postponed with co_nmt_on_tpdo_event_lock().

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the PDO number (in the range [1..512]). If n is 0, the transmission of all PDOs is triggered.

briefdescription.para: Postpones the transmission of PDOs triggered by co_nmt_on_tpdo_event() until a matching call to co_nmt_on_tpdo_event_unlock().

detaileddescription.para: This function can be invoked multiple times. PDO transmission will resume after an equal number of calls to co_nmt_on_tpdo_event_unlock().

briefdescription.para: Undoes the effect of a single call to co_nmt_on_tpdo_event_lock() and possibly triggers the transmission of postponed PDOs.
Returns the pending node-ID.
Sets the pending node-ID.

detaileddescription.para: The node-ID of the device will be updated once the NMT 'reset communication command' is received. This is used for the LSS configure node-ID protocol.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the node-ID of the device (in the range [1..127, 255]).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Returns the current state of a CANopen NMT service (one of CO_NMT_ST_BOOTUP, CO_NMT_ST_STOP, CO_NMT_ST_START, CO_NMT_ST_RESET_NODE, CO_NMT_ST_RESET_COMM or CO_NMT_ST_PREOP).
Returns true if the specified CANopen NMT service is a master, and false if not.
Sets the alternate CAN bus identifier which the NMT redundancy manager service could utilize.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
a CAN bus identifier.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Returns the currently active CAN bus on which the NMT service operates.
Sets the active CAN bus on which the NMT service operates.

detaileddescription.para: Only NMT master can initiate a bus change.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
a CAN bus identifier.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Returns the default SDO timeout used during the NMT 'boot slave' and 'check configuration' processes.
Sets the default SDO timeout used during the NMT 'boot slave' and 'check configuration' processes.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the SDO timeout (in milliseconds). See co_csdo_set_timeout().

briefdescription.para: Submits an NMT request to a slave.

detaileddescription.para: If id equals the node-ID of the master, this is equivalent to co_nmt_cs_ind(nmt, cs).

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the NMT command specifier (one of CO_NMT_CS_START, CO_NMT_CS_STOP, CO_NMT_CS_ENTER_PREOP, CO_NMT_CS_RESET_NODE or CO_NMT_CS_RESET_COMM).
the node-ID (0 for all nodes, [1..127] for a specific slave).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Confirms the completion of the process when booting an NMT master.

detaileddescription.para: The function specified to co_nmt_set_lss_req() MUST cause this function to be invoked.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Requests the NMT 'boot slave' process for the specified node.

detaileddescription.para: The function specified to co_nmt_set_boot_ind() is invoked on completion.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the node-ID (in the range [1..127]).
the SDO timeout (in milliseconds). See co_csdo_set_timeout().

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Returns true if the NMT 'boot slave' process is currently running for the specified node, and false if not.
Checks if a boot-up message has been received from the specified node(s).

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the node-ID (0 for all mandatory nodes, [1..127] for a specific slave).

simplesect.para: 1 if a boot-up message was received, 0 if not, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

memberdef.argsstring: (co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t id, int_least32_t timeout, co_nmt_cfg_con_t *con, void *data)
memberdef.name: co_nmt_cfg_req

briefdescription.para: Issues the NMT 'configuration request' for the specified node.

detaileddescription.para: The function specified to co_nmt_set_cfg_ind() is invoked to complete the request.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the node-ID (0 for all nodes, [1..127] for a specific slave).
the SDO timeout (in milliseconds). See co_csdo_set_timeout().
a pointer to the confirmation function (can be NULL).
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to con.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Indicates the result of the 'update configuration' step of an NMT 'request configuration' request for the specified node (see Fig.

detaileddescription.para: 8 in CiA 302-2 version 4.1.0). This function MUST be called upon completion.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the node-ID (in the range [1..127]).
the SDO abort code (0 on success).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

memberdef.argsstring: (co_nmt_t *nmt, co_unsigned8_t id, co_unsigned16_t gt, co_unsigned8_t ltf)
memberdef.name: co_nmt_ng_req

briefdescription.para: Request the node guarding service for the specified node, even if it is not in the network list.

detaileddescription.para: If the guard time or lifetime factor is 0, node guarding is disabled.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the node-ID (in the range [1..127]).
the guard time (in milliseconds).
the lifetime factor.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Processes an NMT command from the master or the application.

detaileddescription.para: Note that this function MAY trigger a reset of one or more CANopen services and invalidate previously obtained results of co_nmt_get_rpdo(), co_nmt_get_tpdo(), co_nmt_get_ssdo(), co_nmt_get_csdo(), co_nmt_get_sync(), co_nmt_get_time() and/or co_nmt_get_emcy().

This function MUST NOT be invoked during an NMT state transition. It is therefore unsafe to call co_nmt_cs_ind() (or co_nmt_cs_req() with the node-ID of the master) from the co_nmt_cs_ind_t and co_nmt_st_ind_t indication functions.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the NMT command specifier (one of CO_NMT_CS_START, CO_NMT_CS_STOP, CO_NMT_CS_ENTER_PREOP, CO_NMT_CS_RESET_NODE or CO_NMT_CS_RESET_COMM).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Indicates the occurrence of a communication error and invokes the specified error behavior (object 1029:01).

detaileddescription.para: Note that this function MAY invoke co_nmt_cs_ind().

briefdescription.para: Indicates the occurrence of an error event and triggers the error handling process (see Fig.

detaileddescription.para: 12 in CiA 302-2 version 4.1.0). Note that this function MAY invoke co_nmt_cs_ind().

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master service.
the node-ID (in the range [1..127]).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to a Receive-PDO service.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the PDO number (in the range [1..512]).

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to a Transmit-PDO service.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the PDO number (in the range [1..512]).

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to a Server-SDO service.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the SDO number (in the range [1..128]).

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to a Client-SDO service.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an NMT master/slave service.
the SDO number (in the range [1..128]).

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to the SYNC producer/consumer service.
Returns a pointer to the TIME producer/consumer service.
Returns a pointer to the EMCY producer/consumer service.
Returns a pointer to the LSS master/slave service.
This header file is part of the CANopen library; it contains the network management (NMT) declarations.

detaileddescription.para:
